# {{< i18n product_backlog >}} {}


## Purpose of This Document

This Backlog translates the **approved scope and roadmap** into a **prioritized,
executable list of work items**, ensuring that:

- Every backlog item supports ecological value
- All work is traceable to OKR 2.1 and WBS elements
- Execution can follow an agile or hybrid delivery model
- Stage‑Gates and KPIs remain enforceable

This document complements the **WBS** and is used during **Execution and
Monitoring**, while remaining governed by PMBOK baselines.

---

## Backlog Principles

1. **Ecology first** — items that protect ecological confidence have highest
   priority  
2. No backlog item exists without:
   - WBS linkage
   - OKR/KR linkage
3. Items are sized to be:
   - Estimable
   - Testable
   - Reviewable by SMEs
4. Backlog refinement does **not** change scope without formal change control

---

## Backlog Structure

Each backlog item includes:

- Backlog ID
- Title
- Description
- Priority
- WBS Reference
- OKR / KR Reference
- Acceptance Criteria
- Owner

---

## Backlog — Phase 0 & 1 (Strategic Foundation + Ecological MVP)

### Epic E0 — Strategic & Governance Foundation

| ID | Title | Priority | WBS | OKR / KR | Owner |
|----|---------------------|------|------|------|-------|
| BL-01 | Finalize Strategy & Governance docs | High | 1.1 / 1.2 | OKR 2.1 | PM |
| BL-02 | Baseline KPIs and measurement methods | High | 1.2 | KR1–KR5 | PO |
| BL-03 | Approve Stage‑Gate framework | High | 1.2 | OKR 2.1 | Sponsor |

**Acceptance Criteria**

- Documents approved
- Governance baselined

---

### Epic E1 — Environmental Data Preparation

| ID | Title | Priority | WBS | OKR / KR | Owner |
|----|---------------------|------|------|------|-------|
| BL-04 | Ingest AUV images (Field 1) | High | 1.3 | KR3 | Data Eng |
| BL-05 | Prepare metadata and spatial context | High | 1.3 | KR3 | Data Eng |
| BL-06 | Define environmental annotation guidelines | High | 1.3 | KR3 | SME Lead |
| BL-07 | Image quality assessment & filtering | High | 1.3 | KR3 | Data Eng |

**Acceptance Criteria**

- Data completeness verified
- Annotation guidelines approved by SMEs

---

### Epic E2 — Annotation & Labeling (HITL)

| ID | Title | Priority | WBS | OKR / KR | Owner |
|----|---------------------|------|------|------|-------|
| BL-08 | Set up AI‑assisted annotation workflow | High | 1.4 | KR1 | DS Lead |
| BL-09 | Pilot labeling with SMEs (Field 1) | High | 1.4 | KR3 | SME Lead |
| BL-10 | Review inter‑annotator consistency | Medium | 1.4 | KR4 | SME Lead |

**Acceptance Criteria**

- SME sign‑off on labels
- Documented labeling consistency

---

### Epic E3 — AI Model Development (Ecological MVP)

| ID | Title | Priority | WBS | OKR / KR | Owner |
|----|---------------------|------|------|------|-------|
| BL-11 | Train baseline binary screening model | High | 1.4 | KR3 | DS Lead |
| BL-12 | Implement precision‑oriented inference | High | 1.4 | KR3 | DS Lead |
| BL-13 | Generate explainability artifacts | Medium | 1.4 | KR3 | DS Lead |

**Acceptance Criteria**

- Model meets minimum ecological thresholds
- Explainability reviewed by SMEs

---

### Epic E4 — Ecological Validation (Field 1)

| ID | Title | Priority | WBS | OKR / KR | Owner |
|----|---------------------|------|------|------|-------|
| BL-14 | Validate screening results with SMEs | High | 1.5 | KR3 | SME Lead |
| BL-15 | Compute KPI‑3 (Data Protection Index) | High | 1.5 | KR3 | PM |
| BL-16 | Prepare G2 Stage‑Gate package | High | 1.5 | OKR 2.1 | PM |

**Acceptance Criteria**

- KPI‑3 ≥ threshold
- G2 decision recorded

---

## 5. Backlog — Phase 2 & 3 (Generalization + Assetization)

### Epic E5 — Generalization (Second Field)

| ID | Title | Priority | WBS | OKR / KR | Owner |
|----|---------------------|------|------|------|-------|
| BL-17 | Ingest AUV images (Field 2) | Medium | 1.3 | KR4 | Data Eng |
| BL-18 | Validate model on second field | Medium | 1.5 | KR4 | SME Lead |
| BL-19 | Analyze cross‑field consistency | Medium | 1.5 | KR4 | DS Lead |

---

### Epic E6 — Digital Asset Consolidation

| ID | Title | Priority | WBS | OKR / KR | Owner |
|----|---------------------|------|------|------|-------|
| BL-20 | Version datasets and metadata | Medium | 1.6 | KR5 | Data Eng |
| BL-21 | Version model artifacts | Medium | 1.6 | KR5 | DS Lead |
| BL-22 | Prepare reuse readiness report | Medium | 1.6 | KR5 | PM |

---

### Epic E7 — Closure & Knowledge Transfer

| ID | Title | Priority | WBS | OKR / KR | Owner |
|----|---------------------|------|------|------|-------|
| BL-23 | Final documentation & lessons learned | Low | 1.7 | OKR 2.1 | PM |
| BL-24 | Final G3/G4 Stage‑Gate review | Low | 1.7 | OKR 2.1 | Sponsor |

---

## Backlog Prioritization Logic

Priority order is determined by:

1. Ecological risk mitigation
2. Dependency on Stage‑Gates
3. Contribution to KR3 (ecological protection)
4. Enablement of reuse (KR4, KR5)

---

## Backlog Governance Rules

- Backlog refinement may:
  - Reorder items
  - Split items
- Backlog refinement may NOT:
  - Add new scope
  - Remove ecological safeguards
  - Bypass Stage‑Gates

All scope changes require formal change control.

---

## Relationship to Other Artifacts

| Artifact | Relationship |
|--------|-------------|
| WBS | Structural backbone |
| Roadmap | Temporal prioritization |
| OKRs / KPIs | Value measurement |
| PMP | Execution governance |
| Risk Register | Risk‑aware prioritization |
